text:
Market today: Tape is mixed-to-cautious. Tesla sinking hard on a confusing reaction to delivery numbers is dragging sentiment across growth/tech. Labor force participation at a 50-year low adds macro unease - bad for risk appetite, which hits your leveraged chip names hardest.

Your holdings: CRWD is the emergency - down 72.81% and your scanner stop is listed at $590 which is far above current price at $195.65, meaning that stop is stale and useless. Clarify your actual stop immediately. SOXL is down 30.26% and already below its $200 stop - you should have been stopped out; margin amplifies this pain badly. SPAL is new, leveraged, already down 12.74% and sitting just above its $21.38 stop - one bad tick and it's gone.

Watch: Whether Tesla's drop spreads into a broader semi/growth selloff that hits SMH and SOXL harder. Also watch if CRWD holds $192-194 - the scanner shows a pattern there.

Bottom line: Stop management on CRWD, SOXL, and SPAL is the only job today - everything else is noise.
plays: SOXL: Price $177.50 is already well below the $200 stop - if you haven't exited, seriously consider it now; holding a 3X leveraged ETF through a broken stop on margin is a fast way to blow up.
CRWD: Your listed stop of $590 is clearly wrong/stale. Scanner shows support entry $194.11-194.30, stop $192.87 - use that as your real decision line; below $192.87 is an exit signal.
SPAL: Sitting $21.94 with stop at $21.38 - that's a 56-cent cushion on a fast-moving leveraged ETF. Know exactly what you'll do if it hits $21.38; no hesitation allowed on margin.
